_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Open Reduction of Small Joint/ : Open Reduction of Small Joint_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Open Reduction of Small Joint_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Dorsal fracture-dislocation of the proximal interphalangeal (PIP) joint is a relatively common injury that most frequently occurs as a result of axial force applied to an extended finger.

Hyperextension of the PIP joint of the fingers results in a spectrum of injuries, ranging from incomplete disruptions of the volar plate and collateral ligaments to unstable fractures and dislocations.
Ruptures of the volar capsular mechanism usually occur at the base of the middle phalanx, often with small fragments of the middle phalangeal bone.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) - affected limb

claim.label: Post-op X-ray -ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) affected limb

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post Procedure X Ray show the Implant/K-wire?

common_queries: Post-procedure X-ray does not show the implant or K-wire.
Pre-authorization X-ray is missing patient ID, date, or side labelling.
Post-operative X-ray is missing patient ID, date, or side labelling.
Invoice and barcode of implant are missing at claim submission.
Clinical photograph of the patient is missing at pre-authorization.
Post-procedure clinical photograph is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Open reduction of small joints involves using small metal screws or a metal plate and screws.
Open Reduction surgical treatment allows the fracture fragments to be restored as close to their normal position as possible and held there until healing occurs.
This technique allows for a more accurate reduction of the small bone fragments.
